name: Italian Game
description: Quiet development, long-term pressure: Giuoco Piano and Pianissimo.
source: curated repertoire (scripts/repertoires/italian.pgn) + lichess explorer, ratings 1400/1600/1800, speeds blitz/rapid

children.comment: The knight's journey: d2-f1-g3(e3) is the whole middlegame plan.
Rule out ...Bg4 and ...Ng4 before doing anything active.
Space and a luft for the c4 bishop; ...b5 is stopped for good.
Prepares d4 later and gives the bishop the c2 retreat; d3 keeps everything solid meanwhile.
Gain space; the e7 bishop blocks Black's own regrouping.
Solid: protect e4, castle, play c3 and the d2-f1-g3 knight tour.
The point: with the bishop passive on e7 Black cannot fight for d4.
The full centre and no counterplay: this is already a comfortable edge.

variations.name: Giuoco Pianissimo
variations.intro: The modern Italian: c3 and d3, castle, then improve slowly. No forcing lines to memorise - a structure to understand.

variations.name: Two Knights with 4.d3
variations.intro: Against 3...Nf6 the quiet 4.d3 keeps every Italian idea and avoids the theory storm after 4.Ng5 - drill that separately in the Fried Liver.

variations.name: Hungarian and ...d6 setups
variations.intro: The passive defences: 3...Be7 (Hungarian) and early ...d6. White gets the centre for free - take it and keep the pieces on.

variations.name: Légal Trap — punish greedy …Bxd1?? with Nd5#
